title: Climatic constraints to crop production and fertilizer use
abstract:
The alarming food deficits projected for sub-Saharan African from recent production figures make it
mandatory that no effort be spared in reversing the trend of declining yields. Developing better
understanding of such climate-related constraints as high intensity storms, inadequate rainfall or poor
rainfall distribution, high temperatures and high evaporative demands, excessive rainfall and high
erodibility of soils will result in measures that can overcome or mitigate these constraints.
